title: Object 92

{{DISPLAYTITLE:Object 92: "BOTTLE 'O VOID"}}{{CSS|User:Egglord1/TheVoid.css}}{{CSS|content=@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Kode+Mono:wght@400..700&display=swap')}}{{Blurbackground|file=Voidinabottle.jpg}}
{{Audio|file=Lvl18.9-Snowfall.mp3|volume=1|secrettext=Looks like it didn't load.|align=center}} '''Bottle 'o Void'''Bottle 'o Void and Object 92 will be used interchangeably throughout this article. is a relatively uncommon substance commonly found in small bottles throughout the Backrooms.
==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Voidinabottle.jpg|caption=Bottle 'o Void within a yet to be identified level.}} Bottle 'o Void is a liquid substance which comes prepackaged within bottles. This substance is purple in color and has small portions of a sparkling element. Bottle 'o Void instances have been observed to only spawn within dimmer levels, and in exceedingly rare occurrences, [[The Void]]. Bottle 'o Void is incredibly useful for construction purposes throughout the Backrooms, as it has been proven to be able to easily remove geometric matter upon contact with it. Via unknown means, if one is affecting a level's matter it will create small flakes of the material that was eliminated, which will then grow and fill in the missing geometry. Due to the reasons stated prior, it is heavily unadvised for one to drink Bottle 'o Void instances. Stomach acid may fight the Bottle 'o Void at first, though it ''will'' result in most, if not all of one's stomach acid to simply vanish, along with the parietal cells which create it. Causing inability to eat and drink food, which will result in death via malnutrition or dehydration. Though, if one consumes small portions of Bottle 'o Void liquid with [[Object 1|Almond Water]], it will produce psychoactive effects onto the consumer. These psychoactive effects have been observed to produce a wide arrange of hallucinations commonly related to the childhood memories of the affected person. This feeling will cease within five minutes of consumption, though it proves to be an effective method of tending to wanderers suffering from [[Phenomenon 35|the Wretched Cycle]] or depression.
==Usage== Bottle 'o Void is most prominently utilized for building purposes. Though, if the amount of Bottle 'o Void is kept to a minimum, it can be utilized in a wide arrange of medicinal products. These products are produced and sold by [[M.E.G.]], [[Backroom Colonists]], and a wide array of major groups with varying recipes. Bottle 'o Void medicine (commonly referred to as "Void Drops") are small, gummy like products which can assist [[Object 1|Almond Water]] in the process of cleansing one from the [[Phenomenon 35|Wretched Cycle]]. It is heavily advised to consume Almond Water whilst taking Void Drops, as it can prevent stomach acid from being destroyed. Bottle 'o Void products are also sold on the black market as a form of psychoactive drug. This form of Bottle 'o Void product is outlawed by [[M.E.G.]], and if one trades one within M.E.G. quarters then they will be sent to a prison in [[Level 10]] for 1 year. They may also be killed for repeat offenses. Despite this, however, one may have this form of Bottle 'o Void product on their person or even use it on any non-M.E.G. controlled level throughout the Backrooms. Bottle 'o Void could be utilized as a weapon against entities or even people. One can rip the cork off and throw the Bottle 'o Void's liquid at an entity, causing catastrophic damage or even death. Of course, one may even utilize this technique against people which, most of the time, is extremely lethal. If you're attempting to kill an entity, it is advised to throw the liquid into their head. If it has no visible head, throw it in their general direction.
==Locations== Bottle 'o Void instances almost always spawn within dimmer levels, and they may even spawn within [[The Void]]. Though, in extremely rare circumstances, Bottle 'o Void instances may spawn in the main nine. So far, no other levels which are not dark have ever been reported to have spawned Bottle 'o Void instances within them.
